summary: This case study documents a gravity-hinged dip tube for large insulated bottles with an interactive kinematic model and design notes. The canonical article uses author-hosted, commit-pinned figure URLs for long-term stability on pubroot.com. Core technical content focuses on a practical engineering solution with clear documentation and a reproducible demo.
strengths: Excellent practical value for product designers and engineers working on hydration vessels.
High reproducibility due to the interactive demo and well-documented supporting repository.
Clear and well-written narrative explaining the problem, solution, and lessons learned.
Effective use of absolute, commit-pinned URLs for figures enhances long-term stability and verifiability.
weaknesses: The core mechanical story was refined across revisions; novelty is primarily in presentation and hosting discipline rather than new physics.
Some claims about physics and materials are simplified for illustrative purposes, as noted in limitations.
suggestions: Consider adding a brief section in the conclusion that explicitly states the key takeaway for a practitioner encountering a similar design challenge.
A brief mention of any minor code improvements or refinements in the interactive demo would help future reviewers.

claims.text: Consumers expect large hydration bottles to drink cleanly at angle.
claims.source: General knowledge of consumer product expectations for hydration bottles.

claims.text: A common failure mode is a molded straw that is rigid with the lid: the tip lifts out of the pool when the bottle tilts.
claims.source: General knowledge of product design challenges for beverage containers.

claims.text: Drinking generates sub-atmospheric pressure on the order of roughly one foot of water column.
The artifact is a static web page with three synchronized canvases: cap-hinge with gravity pendulum, rigid straw for contrast, and mid-straw flex with a tunable hinge fraction.
claims.source: Review of the provided HTML and JavaScript code.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Deep Search for Joint Sources of Gravitational Waves and High-Energy Neutrinos with IceCube During the Third Observing Run of LIGO and Virgo
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission focuses on a mechanical design problem for consumer products, which is entirely distinct from the physics research presented in this arXiv paper.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Search for High-energy Neutrinos from Binary Neutron Star Merger GW170817 with ANTARES, IceCube, and the Pierre Auger Observatory
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission addresses a practical engineering design challenge for a consumer product, unrelated to astrophysical event detection.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Observation of the rare $B^0_s\to\mu^+\mu^-$ decay from the combined analysis of CMS and LHCb data
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission is about a mechanical design for a hydration bottle, with no relation to particle physics research.
